# Ada Lovelace

Augusta Ada King, Countess of Lovelace, born 10 December 1815 in London,
was a British mathematician and writer chiefly known for her work on
Charles Babbage's proposed mechanical general-purpose computer, the
Analytical Engine.

Ada Lovelace's notes on the Analytical Engine include what is recognised
as the first algorithm intended to be carried out by a machine. As a
result she is often regarded as the first computer programmer.

Ada Lovelace corresponded extensively with Charles Babbage about the
design of the Analytical Engine. Their working relationship is one of
the earliest documented collaborations between a hardware designer and
a software author.

The Ada programming language, named after Ada Lovelace, was developed
by the United States Department of Defense in the late 1970s. The
language is widely used in safety-critical and mission-critical
embedded systems.
